title: Vox Mana
kicker: Commander color identity placement, data modeling, visual UX, and source governance.
problem: Creative identity systems can become vague fast when data, lore, UI, and generated output all blur together.
goal: Build a source-governed Commander color identity placement system that feels useful, visual, and maintainable.
stack: HTML, CSS, JavaScript, generated JSON, visual regression checks, Codex-assisted workflows.
constraints: No confidential data, source-first governance, public copy must stay understandable, generated data must not become source authority.
built: A multi-page static site with Home, Strategium, Archscry, Apocrypha, placement data, identity dossiers, and documented decision layers.
broke: Visual regression drift, route-specific readability issues, generated data boundaries, and AI scope creep all needed explicit guardrails.
learned: Good side projects need the same quality habits as work systems: contracts, checks, scope boundaries, and handoffs.

title: Recipe Flexibility App Concept
kicker: Recipe adaptation for cookware, substitutions, time, and family constraints.
problem: Recipes often assume you have the exact pan, ingredient, oven behavior, time, and skill level they were written for.
goal: Help home cooks adapt recipes safely and practically when real life does not match the recipe card.
stack: Static concept today; future app could use structured recipe parsing, substitution rules, and user constraints.
constraints: Must avoid unsafe cooking advice, must explain uncertainty, and must keep changes simple enough for a parent cooking on a weeknight.
built: A concept model for cookware swaps, time adjustments, ingredient substitutions, and family-friendly constraints.
broke: The hard part is not generating suggestions; it is knowing when a substitution changes the actual cooking method.
learned: Useful AI products need constraint handling more than clever responses.

title: MTG Store Inventory App Concept
kicker: Inventory ingestion, pricing, condition tracking, and local game store support.
problem: Local card inventory is hard to evaluate because condition, pricing, demand, duplicates, and data ingestion all interact.
goal: Explore whether a lightweight app could help a local store understand inventory value, sales opportunity, and operational gaps.
stack: Concept stage; possible future stack includes card APIs, barcode/CSV ingestion, pricing feeds, and condition workflows.
constraints: Inventory data quality, pricing volatility, card condition judgment, and store-specific workflow differences.
built: A product framing around ingestion, normalization, pricing, condition tracking, and decision support.
broke: The moat is not just displaying card data; existing tools already do that. The opportunity is workflow fit and store-level insight.
learned: A product stands out when it solves the messy operational layer, not just the visible lookup problem.
